overview.market_size: US roofing market projected to reach $41.5B by 2034
overview.growth_rate: Stable growth driven by aging infrastructure, storm damage, and re-roofing cycles
overview.fragmentation: Extremely fragmented — thousands of independent contractors. PE platform count tripled from 17 to 56 in just 24 months (2023-2024).
overview.typical_ebitda_margin: 12%–20% for well-run operators; service/maintenance-focused businesses at higher end
overview.recurring_revenue_note: Service and maintenance programs create recurring revenue streams that command 1-2x EBITDA premium over construction-heavy counterparts.
overview.pe_interest: Explosive — PE platform count grew 229% in 24 months. 134 contractors acquired in 2024 (up 25% from 106 in 2023). Deals occurring at near-weekly cadence.
overview.labor_challenge: Skilled labor shortage — roofing is physically demanding with high turnover
overview.ai_disruption_risk: Very Low — physical roofing work resistant to automation. Drone inspections emerging but complementary.
overview.landmark_deals: Home Depot acquired SRS Distribution for ~$18B (March 2024); QXO acquired Beacon Building Products for $10.6B (March 2025)

$2M-$5M.source: Axial / First Page Sage / AXIA Advisors
$2M-$5M.notes: Axial reports typical range of 4.3x–7.0x. Companies that would have sold for 5x a decade ago now command 6-7x.

$5M-$10M.notes: Multiples came down from peak of 8x-11x to more sustainable 6x-9x range in 2024 as platforms focused on integration.

$10M-$15M.notes: Well-positioned businesses with service revenue, multi-market presence, and strong management can achieve 8-10x.

$15M-$25M.notes: Public roofing and building products companies trade at ~12.9x median, suggesting substantial room for private valuation expansion.

value_drivers.explanation: Service-focused businesses with strong maintenance programs command 1-2x EBITDA premium over construction-heavy counterparts. Predictable cash flows are key.
If a company can prove out greenfield growth and validate 4-12 markets, valuations can increase towards double digits. Demonstrates scalability.
Commercial roofing contracts are larger, longer-term, and less weather-dependent than residential storm chasing.
PE firms require management teams that can operate independently. Transition risk is heavily scrutinized.
Clean books, accurate job costing, and auditable financials signal professionalism. Many roofing contractors have poor financial reporting.
Storm-chasing roofing companies face revenue volatility. Buyers discount for weather-dependent business models.
W-2 model provides operational control; sub model offers flexibility. Buyers assess labor classification risk.

recent_transactions.rationale: Largest roofing distribution deal in history. Home Depot entering professional contractor supply chain.
Major distribution consolidation. Board initially resisted but agreed to terms.
PE-backed roofing platforms acquired 134 contractors in 2024, up 25% from 106 in 2023.
At least 19 roofing companies changed hands in Q1 2024 alone — near-weekly cadence.

pe_platforms.name: Various PE sponsors
pe_platforms.platform: 56 active roofing PE platforms (as of end-2024)
pe_platforms.thesis: Roofing platform count tripled from 17 to 56 in 2023-2024. Each platform actively seeking add-on acquisitions.
pe_platforms.geographic_focus: National — concentration in Sun Belt and storm-prone regions

pe_platforms.name: Radnor Roofing (PE-backed)
pe_platforms.thesis: Active commercial and residential roofing platform with multi-market strategy

strategic.name: Tecta America
strategic.strategy: Largest US commercial roofing contractor — active acquirer of commercial roofing businesses

strategic.name: CentiMark Corporation
strategic.strategy: Major commercial roofing and flooring contractor — acquisition-driven growth
